content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian.We define the GCD set of an array AAA of length NNN as the set containing the GCD of all prefixes of AAA. More formally, the GCD set GGG of AAA contains the values gcd⁡([A1]),gcd⁡([A1,A2]),gcd⁡([A1,A2,A3]),…,gcd⁡([A1,A2,…,An])\gcd([A_1]), \gcd([A_1, A_2]), \gcd([A_1, A_2, A_3]), \ldots, \gcd([A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_n])gcd([A1​]),gcd([A1​,A2​]),gcd([A1​,A2​,A3​]),…,gcd([A1​,A2​,…,An​]), where gcd⁡(B)\gcd(B)gcd(B) is the largest integer ddd such that all the numbers in array BBB are divisible by ddd. Note that **all values in GGG are distinct**.

For example, gcd⁡([6])=6\gcd([6]) = 6gcd([6])=6, gcd⁡([6,12])=6\gcd([6, 12]) = 6gcd([6,12])=6, gcd⁡([6,12,9])=3\gcd([6, 12, 9]) = 3gcd([6,12,9])=3, and gcd⁡([6,12,9,2])=1\gcd([6, 12, 9, 2]) = 1gcd([6,12,9,2])=1. Therefore, the GCD set of the array [6,12,9,2][6, 12, 9, 2][6,12,9,2] is {1,3,6}\{1, 3, 6\}{1,3,6}.

You are given an integer set SSS of size MMM and two integers N,MODN, MODN,MOD, where MODMODMOD is a prime. You need to calculate the probability that the GCD set of PPP is equal to SSS, where PPP is a **permutation** of values 111 to NNN chosen uniformly randomly.

It can be shown that the answer is in the form of PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ where PPP and QQQ are coprime non-negative integers and Q≠0Q \neq 0Q=0. You need to find the value P⋅Q−1(modMOD)P \cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{MOD}P⋅Q−1(modMOD).


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains three integers N,M,MODN, M, MODN,M,MOD denoting the length of the permutation, the size of the given integer set, and the modulo respectively.
The second line of each test case contains mmm integers in increasing order S1,S2,…,SMS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_MS1​,S2​,…,SM​ representing the given integer set.


Output Format
For each test case, output on one line an integer equals to the answer of that test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, there are 2 permutations that satisfy the condition:



[4,2,1,3][4, 2, 1, 3][4,2,1,3]


[4,2,3,1][4, 2, 3, 1][4,2,3,1]


Since there are 4!4!4! permutations of length 444 in total, the probability is 24!=112\frac{2}{4!} = \frac{1}{12}4!2​=121​. As 83333334⋅12≡1(mod109+7)83333334 \cdot 12 \equiv 1 \pmod {10^9 + 7}83333334⋅12≡1(mod109+7), we output 833333348333333483333334.

For the second test case, the probability is 19\frac{1}{9}91​.
